binding_spec_verbatim.text: (1) PHOTO PACKS — PWYW-vs-$5, a $3 anchor, and a two-pack bundle (the packs themselves are hard-gated on owner-held originals; the pricing verdict is serveable now);

packet_request_verbatim.source: menno420/venture-lab control/outbox.md @ 847b636f174d439949afeffac55025dde814514b, section '2026-07-13 · night-run MORNING TALLY (coordinator → manager, ORDER 008)'
packet_request_verbatim.text: SIM-REQUEST: photo packs PWYW-vs-$5 + $3 anchor + two-pack bundle · bundle $59 vs $64/$68 anchors · $19 fixed vs PWYW for narrow-TAM cookbooks · owner sandbox repo to production-verify merge-on-green.yml.

packet_question_verbatim.text: Genuine uncertainty flagged as SIM-REQUEST (via outbox lane, not this packet): (1) PWYW vs fixed $5 conversion for cold wallpaper packs; (2) whether saturated golden-hours should anchor at $3 instead; (3) two-pack bundle price. No cited evidence either way — defaults above stand until simulated or measured.

hard_gate_note_verbatim.text: HARD-GATED honest: $59 Ship-It Bundle (on ⚑B/⚑D clicks), photo packs (owner-held originals).
hard_gate_note_verbatim.scope: the ORDER 006 item itself states 'the packs themselves are hard-gated on owner-held originals; the pricing verdict is serveable now' — this verdict prices, it does not publish

default_price_usd.source_verbatim: Recommendation: $5 fixed per pack, both packs, Gumroad direct (packs.json `price_usd: 5`; MARKET-PLAN channel (a) range $3-10).

owner_queue_price_default.source_verbatim: ⚑ Price: $5 fixed per pack (default — §3 evidence; floor $3; PWYW is an open SIM-REQUEST, not tonight's default).

gumroad_direct_fees.source_verbatim: Gumroad: flat 10% platform fee + $0.50 per transaction on direct sales; payment processing (~2.9% + $0.30) brings the practical total to roughly ~12.9% + $0.80 per sale.

gumroad_discover_fees.source_verbatim: Products surfaced via Gumroad's Discover marketplace are charged 30% (processing included).

kofi_free_fees.source_verbatim: Ko-fi: 0% on tips; 5% on Shop / Memberships / Commissions on the free plan (plus normal ~3% + $0.30 processor fee).

packet_worked_nets.values: $5 Gumroad direct ≈ $3.56 · Gumroad Discover $3.50 · Ko-fi free ≈ $4.30
packet_worked_nets.source_verbatim: Worked nets from the cited fee schedules: $5 on Gumroad direct ≈ $3.56/sale; Gumroad Discover ≈ $3.50; Ko-fi free plan ≈ $4.30.
packet_worked_nets.reconciliation_note: exact fractions: direct net(5) = 711/200 = 3.555 (the packet's $3.56 rounds up); Discover 7/2 = 3.50 exact; Ko-fi 43/10 = 4.30 exact — the sim decides on the exact values and reports both

fee_floor_rule.source_verbatim: Below ~$3 the fixed fees ($0.50 + ~$0.30) eat >25% of price — do not price under $3.
fee_floor_rule.reconciliation_note: exact: fixed fees 0.80/p > 25% iff p < 3.20 (16/5); at the $3 anchor itself the fixed-fee share is 4/15 = 26.67% — already above the packet's own 25% line

demand_expectation.source_verbatim: Conservative expectation stays $0–30 / first 90 days cold (MARKET-PLAN header; the cited $10-total creator anecdote is the realistic anchor, not the $1000-with-audience one).

pack_lineup.value: 2 packs with committed previews: dutch-skies (3 samples) leads, golden-hours (4 samples) most-saturated theme ('do not lead with it'); registry price_usd: 5 on every pack

storefront_default.source_verbatim: ⚑ Storefront pick: Gumroad (default — MARKET-PLAN channel (a) default; Discover gives audience-free category browse) or Ko-fi (better per-sale net ≈ $4.30 vs $3.56, no marketplace browse) — owner's call.
storefront_default.decision_channel_note: the DECISION is taken on Gumroad direct (the D6 default); Ko-fi and Discover frontiers ship as reporting columns

assumed_not_packet_pinned: A1 BUNDLE WEAK-DOMINANCE FRAME: offering a bundle listing does not reduce the total buyer count (no choice-overload effect); buyers partition into would-buy-both / would-buy-one / would-not-buy, and adding an option only moves buyers between options. Rationale: standard discrete-choice monotonicity; no packet datum either way — flagged, and the conservative fresh-buyer rate f = 0 is used for the decision leg.

A2 PROCESSOR RATE FIXED at the packet's own cited values (2.9% + $0.30 direct; 3% + $0.30 Ko-fi). The packet itself says 'per-sale net is exact only for a given processor rate' — a ±0.6pp processing-rate sensitivity column ships as reporting.

A3 PWYW MECHANICS: a PWYW taker either pays w ≥ floor or takes at $0; per-transaction fees apply only to paid transactions; a $0 take nets exactly $0 (no fee owed on a $0 transaction — not packet-pinned, marked ASSUMED).

A4 GRID SPANS ARE REGISTERED BANDS, NOT MEASUREMENTS: u ∈ [0,3], w̄ ∈ [0,10], ρ ∈ [1,3], t,g ∈ [0,1], b ∈ [7,10] are sweep bounds chosen to bracket every packet-plausible value (price band $3–10 is the packet's own channel (a) range); no probability mass is claimed anywhere on them.
recorded_gaps_feeding_null_or_conditiona: G1: NO measured PWYW conversion rate, taker rate, or mean voluntary payment for cold wallpaper packs — the packet says verbatim 'No cited evidence either way'.
G2: NO measured price-elasticity datum between $3 and $5 (the anchor question) — same packet sentence.
G3: NO measured bundle mix (share of two-pack buyers, upgrade propensity, fresh-buyer pull) — same packet sentence ('(3) two-pack bundle price').
G4: NO absolute demand data — the packet's own scale is '$0–30 / first 90 days cold' with the $10-total anecdote as the realistic anchor; no sales projection is made anywhere in this verdict.
G5: processor rates are cited approximations ('~2.9%', '~3%') — the packet's own exactness caveat; direction and magnitude bracketed in a reporting column.
G6: the sellable artifact does not exist (owner-held originals, PACK-SPEC safety rule) — pricing is serveable now per the order text; nothing here unblocks the hard gate.

model.frame: expected net revenue per committed baseline buyer (one visitor who would buy exactly one pack at the $5 default on the picked channel — the packet's own per-sale frame extended one step); exact rational arithmetic (fractions of USD) on every decision quantity

arms.FIXED_5: the packet default D7: $5 fixed per pack. E[net] = net(5) per baseline buyer. Zero unmeasured parameters on a given channel.
arms.PWYW: pay-what-you-want listing: u = paid PWYW transactions per baseline $5 buyer (taker-multiplier × paid-conversion, folded), w̄ = mean paid amount. E[net] = u · net(w̄) by linearity of net(·) over paid transactions ($0 takes net $0, A3). Both u and w̄ unmeasured (G1).
arms.ANCHOR_3: price the pack at $3 (the golden-hours anchor question, §3 SIM-REQUEST (2)). E[net] = ρ · net(3), ρ = n3/n5 = unit-sales ratio at $3 vs $5. ρ unmeasured (G2).
arms.BUNDLE_2PACK: add a two-pack bundle listing at price b alongside the $5 singles. Per baseline buyer with mix (t = share who would buy both packs anyway, g = share of remaining one-pack buyers who upgrade, f = fresh buyers per baseline buyer): ΔE[net] vs no-bundle = t·(net(b) − 2·net(5)) + (1−t)·g·(net(b) − net(5)) + f·net(b). Mix unmeasured (G3); decision leg at the conservative f = 0.

sweep_grids.pwyw_grid: u ∈ 0.00..3.00 step 0.10 (31) × w̄ ∈ 0.00..10.00 step 0.50 (21) = 651 cells, WIN map PWYW vs FIXED_5, all three channels (decision column: Gumroad direct)
sweep_grids.anchor_grid: ρ ∈ 1.00..3.00 step 0.05 (41 points), WIN iff ρ·net(3) ≥ net(5), all three channels
sweep_grids.bundle_grid: b ∈ {8.00, 8.50, 9.00, 9.50, 9.99, 10.00} × t ∈ 0.00..1.00 step 0.05 (21) × g ∈ 0.00..1.00 step 0.05 (21) at f = 0 → WIN map ΔE[net] ≥ 0, Gumroad direct decision + Ko-fi/Discover reporting
sweep_grids.processing_sensitivity: REPORTING-ONLY, cannot flip the decision: direct-processing rate ∈ {2.3%, 2.9%, 3.5%} — re-renders net(5), net(3), b* per rate
sweep_grids.scale_scenarios: REPORTING-ONLY: 90-day units n5 ∈ {0, 2, 6} (the packet's $0 / $10-anchor / $30-cap gross band at $5) rendered per arm

decision_rule.note: pre-registered BEFORE the runner; evaluated strictly in order; all breakevens exact fractions on the Gumroad-direct decision channel
order: R1 PWYW-WINS: iff the packet carries a MEASURED pair (û, ŵ) with û·net(ŵ) ≥ net(5) → replace the $5 default with PWYW. (Registered though unreachable by the packet's own text — G1 says no such measurement exists; kept so a future packet re-reads.)

R2 ANCHOR-WINS: iff the packet carries a MEASURED unit-sales ratio ρ̂ = n3/n5 with ρ̂·net(3) ≥ net(5) (exact bar: ρ̂ ≥ 3555/1813 ≈ 1.9608) → drop the anchor pack to $3. (Registered though unreachable — G2.)

R3 CONDITIONAL-DEFAULT: iff NO measured conversion/elasticity/mix datum exists in the packet (G1/G2/G3 all open) AND the bundle dominance band [b*, 10] is non-empty on the decision channel (b* := price where net(b*) = 2·net(5)) → keep the packet's own $5-fixed default (the only arm with zero unmeasured parameters) AND additionally offer the two-pack bundle priced INSIDE [b*, $10] (weak dominance under A1 at f = 0: every mix cell (t, g) is non-losing, and the saved second per-transaction fixed fee is a strict gain on every two-pack buyer); PWYW parks behind the measured (u, w̄) frontier; the $3 anchor parks behind the measured ρ bar; any bundle price BELOW b* parks behind the measured mix frontier.
R4 NULL: anything else — name the binding gap.
decision_rule.honest_null_note: R3 and R4 are both honest outcomes; the frontier tables are the citable pin either way. No sales projection is made anywhere (the packet's own '$0–30 / first 90 days cold' stands).

expected_exact_values_hand_derived.anchor_ratio_bar_direct: net(5)/net(3) = 3555/1813 ≈ 1.960838 (the $3 anchor needs +96.1% unit sales to tie)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.pwyw_parity_wbar_at_u1_direct: w̄* = 5 exactly (linearity: u = 1 and mean payment $5 ties the $5 default)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.pwyw_u_bar_at_wbar3_direct: 3555/1813 ≈ 1.9608 (identical to the anchor bar — PWYW averaging the $3 floor IS $3 pricing)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.bundle_bstar_discover: 10 exactly (no fixed fee on Discover → zero demand-free discount room)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.bundle_net_at_9.99_direct: 790129/100000 = 7.90129 (+0.79129 vs two singles = the saved fixed fee minus the 1-cent discount's 0.871 share)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.bundle_net_at_10_direct: 791/100 = 7.91 (+0.80 exactly = the saved second per-transaction fixed fee)
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.bundle_net_at_8_direct: 771/125 = 6.168 (−0.942 per cannibalized two-pack buyer; +2.613 per upgraded one-pack buyer; frontier g ≥ 0.942·t / (2.613·(1−t)))
expected_exact_values_hand_derived.fee_floor_crossing: fixed-fee share 0.80/p = 25% at p = 16/5 = 3.20; at the $3 anchor the share is 4/15 ≈ 26.67% — the packet's own do-not-price-under line, reproduced exactly

registered.20260790: robustness leg A — 2,000 uniform draws (u, w̄) over the registered PWYW bands; gate: expected-value evaluator and frontier-inequality evaluator must agree in sign on every draw
registered.20260791: robustness leg B — 2,000 uniform draws (b, t, g) over the registered bundle bands; same twin-evaluator sign-agreement gate

seeds.allocation_note: strictly above the recorded fleet high-water 20260775 (VERDICT 037's registration line: 'the seed high-water mark 20260775 is therefore untouched'); a deliberate +14 gap is left below 20260790 for the two in-flight sibling slices holding RESERVED numbers VERDICT 036 and VERDICT 038, so no sibling seed collision is possible; new high-water claimed: 20260791
seeds.scope: the robustness legs are GATES on the exact frontier logic, reporting-only — the decision arm is exact Fraction arithmetic and no seed can move the ruling

determinism_contract: stdout and results.json byte-identical across two full process runs, proven by external diff; all JSON keys sorted; no timestamps inside results.json; stdlib-only; the sim reads exactly ONE file (this fixtures.json)
